title: What is Job-Hopper?
content: Job-Hopper is a subscription-based job matching service and web app built for job seekers—not employers. Think of it as the AI tool that does your job search for you: we continuously scan and curate roles from across the web, then use your profile, preferences, and career level to deliver only the opportunities that make sense for you.

title: How is this different from job boards like Indeed or LinkedIn?
content: Most job boards are funded by and built to serve the interests of employers. Companies pay to post or promote roles, and candidates do the heavy lifting—searching, applying, and hoping something sticks. Job-Hopper flips that model. Because our service is paid for by subscribers, we work exclusively on your behalf: we filter and enrich roles, screen out obvious 'ghost' or low-activity postings, and match only the best fits to your profile so you're not stuck sorting through endless noise.

title: Who pays for Job-Hopper—and whose side are you on?
content: Job-Hopper is paid for by job seekers through subscriptions, not by employers. That means our incentives are aligned with you. Our job is to save you time, cut down on wasted effort, and surface quality roles that are actually worth your attention—not to drive more clicks or applicants to paid job ads.

title: Do you apply to jobs for me?
content: No. You stay in control of your applications. Job-Hopper surfaces opportunities and context for each role, and you decide where, how, and when to apply.

title: Can you guarantee I'll get a job?
content: We can't guarantee a specific outcome—no honest service can. What we can do is save you time, reduce frustration, and dramatically improve the quality, timing, and relevance of the roles you pursue.

title: Where do the jobs come from?
content: We pull from public job boards, direct company career pages, and other online sources. Our automation engine continuously scans for new postings, then our rules and team review them for freshness, legitimacy, and signs of real hiring activity before they ever reach your feed.

title: Is Job-Hopper only for certain industries?
content: No. Job-Hopper is built for anyone in the United States who is serious about their next move. Our automation engine searches and matches across industries from your profile and preferences. We add extra human vetting where we see the densest, noisiest markets—today that includes manufacturing and tech, especially analytics and engineering-heavy roles—so matches stay sharp. For other fields (healthcare, retail leadership, logistics, professional services, and more) the same engine still works effectively; niche or highly unusual titles may see fewer postings simply because the market is thinner, but we keep searching on your behalf.

title: Which industries do you cover today?
content: Today the product spans the economy—from desk jobs to the plant floor and everything in between. We are seeing standout results for people in tech and manufacturing, particularly analytics and engineering paths such as Data Analyst, Systems Analyst, Full Stack Developer, Mechanical Engineer, Process Engineer, Supply Chain Analyst, Manufacturing Systems Analyst, and IT Support Specialist. In those busy segments we pair AI matching with extra human review to keep signal high. Healthcare, logistics, retail leadership, professional services, and other sectors already run on the same engine, and we keep deepening coverage as we grow.

title: Which locations do you support?
content: Currently, Job-Hopper focuses on roles based in the United States. In your profile, you can set preferred locations, indicate whether you're open to relocation, include or exclude remote options, and adjust those settings anytime.

title: How often will I get new matches?
content: It depends on your profile, preferences, and the current market. Many users see new matches weekly. Our priority is quality over volume, so you may receive a smaller number of strong, well-matched opportunities rather than a constant stream of random postings.

title: Can I change my preferences later?
content: Yes. You can update your target roles, salary expectations, locations, industries of interest, and other settings at any time from your Job-Hopper dashboard. Your future matches will automatically adjust as you refine your profile.

title: What is the sponsorship-likelihood signal?
content: For roles that flow through the Hopper, Job-Hopper runs thorough analysis of posting and employer metadata to produce a sponsorship-likelihood estimate—useful when you need to prioritize openings where employer visa sponsorship (for example H-1B) is more plausible. It is a filter and a time-saver, not a guarantee that any specific role or company will sponsor. Many subscribers never turn it on; it is there when your situation calls for it.

title: How does the free trial work?
content: Every base plan starts with a free trial. During the trial, you get access to your job feed and email alerts so you can judge the quality and relevance of your matches for yourself. If you don't want to continue, simply cancel before the trial ends and you won't be charged.

title: Are resume add-ons included in the free trial?
content: Your free trial focuses on the core Job-Hopper experience: curated matches, your personal feed, and email alerts. Optional resume services—such as a one-time resume upgrade or per-job tailoring—are billed separately when you purchase them.

title: Why are there different base plans?
content: Base plans are priced by role level and job type—not by features. Entry & Mid Level, Senior & Management, and Director/VP/C-Level plans all include the same core service. The pricing reflects the level of roles you're targeting and the narrower, more specialized searches that occur at higher tiers.

title: Do higher-priced tiers come with different features?
content: No. All three tiers share the same core Job-Hopper experience: curated matches, your job feed, and email delivery of new opportunities. Optional resume services are separate add-ons you can purchase when you want them.

title: Can I change plans later?
content: Yes. If your career level or target roles change—for example, you move from mid-level to management or step into an executive search—you can upgrade or downgrade your base plan from your account settings. Changes typically apply to the next billing cycle.

title: Do I have to buy add-ons to get value?
content: No. The base plans are designed to stand on their own. Most users will see strong value from curated, well-matched opportunities alone. Resume add-ons are there if you want help refreshing your resume or tailoring it for a specific role.

title: What happens if I find a job?
content: First—congratulations. You can cancel your subscription in a couple of clicks or keep it active if you want to stay aware of future opportunities or quietly monitor the market. Many users pause, then come back when they're ready for their next move.

title: Do employers see my profile?
content: No. Job-Hopper is not a public candidate database, and we do not give employers access to browse or search your profile. We use your information internally to power matching and recommendations. If we ever introduce features that allow selective visibility to employers, they will be strictly opt-in and under your control.

title: Do you share my data with employers or third parties?
content: No. We do not share your personal profile data with employers or sell it to third-party advertisers. Any information an employer receives about you comes directly from you—through the applications you choose to submit and any outreach you decide to send. Our role is to help you find and prioritize opportunities, not to trade on your data.

title: How do you protect my data?
content: We use industry-standard security practices to safeguard your information and we never sell your personal data to advertisers. Your profile exists to help us match you with better jobs—nothing else. For full details on how we handle and protect your information, see our Privacy Policy.

title: I still have questions—how do I contact you?
content: You can reach us anytime at support@job-hopper.io or by using the contact form on our Support page. We typically respond within 1–2 business days.

title: Why are there different prices?
content: Because the plans differ in how much Job-Hopper automates for you — not by seniority or job type. Free lets you search manually with capped access and teaser insights. Core adds unlimited automated matching, email digests, full insights, full resume advice, and an application tracker. Premium (coming soon) layers on a deeper sponsorship intelligence set.

title: Do higher tiers come with different features?
content: Yes — that's the whole point. Core unlocks automated daily matching, the full sponsorship badge, full Premium Insights, full Resume Advice, and the application tracker. Premium (coming soon) adds the real sponsorship intelligence layer: Real Sponsorship Score, Sponsor Watch, Apply Intelligence, hiring manager contact, and the Ghost Listing Detector.

title: Can I change tiers later?
content: Yes. You can upgrade or downgrade your plan from your dashboard at any time as your needs change.

title: Do I have to buy add-ons to get value?
content: No. Core stands on its own. The resume add-ons — a one-time resume upgrade and per-job resume tailoring — are there only if you want extra help, and they're unaffected by your plan tier.

title: Is there a free trial?
content: Yes — Core starts with a free trial, so you can see the quality and relevance of your matches before you commit. Premium isn't purchasable yet, so there's no trial for it; join the waitlist and we'll notify you the moment it launches.

title: How do billing and cancellation work?
content: Core is billed monthly and you can cancel at any time in a couple of clicks from your account settings. Resume add-ons are one-time purchases billed at checkout, not recurring subscription charges.

title: Simple plans, priced by depth — not seniority.
content:
Job-Hopper gives every user the same core service: curated, high-quality job matches delivered to your inbox and dashboard, powered by AI and human vetting.

Pick the tier that matches how much you want automated for you.

title: Every plan starts with the same core advantage.
content:
No matter which tier you choose, Job-Hopper gives you:

The same curated matching, dashboard feed, and optional tools such as sponsorship-likelihood signals when they are relevant to your search—still fundamentally an AI that runs your job search, not a niche sponsorship-only service.

Curated, vetted job matches instead of endless scrolling

Matching powered by automation engine plus human review

A personal job feed in your dashboard

Email delivery of new opportunities

title: How Job-Hopper finds the right jobs for you
content:
Job-Hopper is the AI layer that runs your U.S. job search — so you're not refreshing five

sites every night.

We scan job boards and company postings, vet each role for freshness and real hiring

activity, then match what's left against your profile, preferences, and career level. You

see company size and background on every posting, so you're never applying blind.

title: Build your profile (60 seconds)
content:
- Your current role and experience level.
- The kinds of jobs you’re targeting.
- Your pay range and preferred locations.
- Your resume, so we understand your background.

title: The Hopper collects and curates
content:
We continuously pull new postings from LinkedIn, Indeed, and company career pages, then:

- Filter out stale, low-quality, and resume-collecting posts.
- Enrich each role with company size, industry, and background.
- Map roles to experience levels.
- Analyze posting metadata to estimate sponsorship likelihood.

title: We match and send
content:
Every new job is checked against your profile for:

- Role alignment and experience level.
- Location and relocation settings.
- Salary or wage expectation.
- Sponsorship likelihood, when you use that filter.

Good fits land in your feed and — depending on your settings — trigger an email or notification.

title: You apply and follow up
content:
- A short AI-generated job briefing.
- A direct link to apply on the company’s platform.

On Premium (rolling out): verified hiring contact info.

title: Not too tight, not too loose
content:
Too tight and you miss opportunities; too loose and you're back in job-board chaos. We

start with a sensible range, then hand you the controls:

- Widen or narrow your job types
- Expand or tighten location boundaries
- Add or remove industries
- Pause certain types of roles
- Toggle sponsorship-likelihood filtering

title: About the sponsorship signal
content:
When visa sponsorship is part of your situation, our sponsorship-likelihood signal helps

you spend less time on postings that are unlikely to line up. It is a practical filter and

estimate — not a promise that a given employer will sponsor.

title: Your data, your control
content:
- We use your profile only to match you with jobs and improve recommendations.
- We never sell your personal data to third-party advertisers.
- You can delete your account at any time.

title: Terms of Service
content:
Welcome to Job-Hopper. These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of the

Job-Hopper website, web application, and related services (collectively, the "Service")

operated by Job-Hopper, a product of Schepmont Group LLC ("Company", "we", "our", or

"us"). By accessing or using Job-Hopper, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you

do not agree, do not use the Service.

title: Description of Service
content:
Job-Hopper is a subscription-based job matching and career support platform designed

to help job seekers discover curated employment opportunities.

The Service may include:

- Curated job listings
- Matching based on user profile and preferences
- Notifications of new opportunities
- Job insights and summaries
- Resume improvement and per-job resume tailoring (optional add-ons)
- Other related features

Job-Hopper does not function as an employer, recruiter, or employment agency unless

explicitly stated.

title: No Employment Guarantee
content:
Job-Hopper does not guarantee employment, interviews, or hiring outcomes.

We provide:

- Curated job opportunities
- Matching tools
- Career insights

You remain solely responsible for:

- Applying to jobs
- Communicating with employers
- Interviewing
- Accepting employment

We do not guarantee:

- Job availability
- Job accuracy
- Hiring outcomes
- Salary levels
- Employer response

title: User Accounts
content:
To use Job-Hopper, you may be required to create an account.

You agree to:

- Provide accurate information
- Keep login credentials secure
- Not share your account
- Notify us of unauthorized access

You are responsible for activity under your account.

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts for misuse.

title: Subscription & Billing
content:
Job-Hopper is offered as a subscription service.

By subscribing, you agree:

- You will be billed recurring monthly charges
- Charges are processed through Stripe
- Subscription automatically renews unless canceled

Pricing tiers may include:

- Entry & Mid Level
- Senior & Management
- Director / Executive

Optional add-ons may include:

- Resume upgrade (one-time)
- Per-job resume tailoring (one-time per role)

Prices may change with notice.

title: Free Trial
content:
If a free trial is offered:

- You will not be charged during trial period
- Billing begins automatically after trial
- You may cancel anytime before trial ends

Failure to cancel before trial expiration results in automatic billing.

title: Cancellation
content:
You may cancel your subscription at any time.

Upon cancellation:

- You retain access until end of billing period
- No prorated refunds unless required by law

To cancel:

- Log into your account
- Navigate to billing settings
- Select cancel subscription

title: Refund Policy
content:
Due to the digital nature of the service:

- Payments are generally non-refundable
- Refunds may be granted at our discretion
- Abuse of refund requests may result in account termination

title: Job Listing Accuracy
content:
Job-Hopper aggregates and curates job listings from third-party sources.

We do not guarantee:

- Accuracy of job postings
- Employer legitimacy
- Job availability
- Salary accuracy

Users should independently verify job details.

title: Third-Party Services
content:
Job-Hopper may integrate with:

- Job boards
- Company websites
- Stripe (payments)
- Analytics providers

We are not responsible for third-party services.

Use of those services is subject to their terms.

title: Acceptable Use
content:
You agree not to:

- Use the service unlawfully
- Attempt to scrape data
- Reverse engineer systems
- Abuse contact information
- Harass employers or other users
- Use automation to exploit the platform

Violation may result in termination.

title: Intellectual Property
content:
All Job-Hopper content is owned by:

Schepmont Group LLC

Including:

- Branding
- Software
- Matching systems
- Content
- Design

You may not copy, reproduce, or redistribute without permission.

title: Disclaimer of Warranties
content:
Job-Hopper is provided "as is" and "as available."

We make no warranties regarding:

- Service availability
- Accuracy
- Reliability
- Results

Use of Job-Hopper is at your own risk.

title: Limitation of Liability
content:
To the fullest extent permitted by law:

Job-Hopper shall not be liable for:

- Employment outcomes
- Lost wages
- Missed opportunities
- Indirect damages
- Consequential damages

Maximum liability shall not exceed fees paid within the last 12 months.

title: Termination
content:
We may suspend or terminate accounts:

- For misuse
- Fraud
- Violation of Terms
- Legal compliance

Users may terminate at any time.

title: Changes to Terms
content:
We may update these Terms periodically.

Updates will be posted on this page.

Continued use constitutes acceptance.

content:
These Terms are governed by the laws of:

United States

Without regard to conflict of law principles.
